element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player chooses from 12 NFL-licensed teams with different roster strengths
Player learns 4-play offensive playbook; most teams have 2 running + 2 passing plays
Player must anticipate opponent's play choice; correct prediction collapses line and covers receivers
Defense avoids blockers or tackles ball carrier directly
Result of correctly anticipating opponent's passing play
Teams like San Francisco and Miami have 3 passing plays vs 1 running play; requires adaptation
One-player mode awards password after each championship win; enables campaign continuation

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based play selection creates rhythmic tension peaks around each snap; intensity builds toward goal line; series of consecutive wins escalates championship difficulty

difficulty_curve.early_game: Initial team selection determines baseline difficulty; player learns 4-play playbook depth
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Computer opponent adaptability increases; player faces varied team-specific strategies (San Francisco/Miami 3-pass formations)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Championship progression requires consecutive victories; each win triggers stronger computer opponent selection
notable_spikes: San Francisco and Miami teams with asymmetric play distributions (3 passing vs 1 running)
Championship game series requiring multiple consecutive victories

notable_level_patterns.description: Each game is self-contained; player selects play, computer selects play, collision resolution determines outcome
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any individual game in one-player or two-player mode

notable_level_patterns.description: One-player mode sequences victories into campaign; password system enables progression tracking across play sessions
Core mechanic: player predicts opponent's play; correct prediction = defensive advantage (sack/interception potential); incorrect = offensive success

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Tecmo Bowl NES)
